title: How to Speak So That People Want to Listen | Julian Treasure | TED

transcript.chinese: 人声：这是我们大家演奏的乐器。
transcript.english: The human voice: It's the instrument we all play.

transcript.chinese: 这可能是世界上最有力的声音。
transcript.english: It's the most powerful sound in the world, probably.

transcript.chinese: 它是唯一一个可以发动战争或说“我爱你”的人。然而，许多人都有这样的经历：当他们说话时，人们不听他们的。
transcript.english: It's the only one that can start a war or say "I love you." And yet many people have the experience that when they speak, people don't listen to them.

transcript.chinese: 这是为什么呢？
transcript.english: And why is that?

transcript.chinese: 我们怎样才能发出有力的声音来改变世界？
transcript.english: How can we speak powerfully to make change in the world?

transcript.chinese: 我想建议的是，我们需要改掉很多习惯。
transcript.english: What I'd like to suggest, there are a number of habits that we need to move away from.

transcript.chinese: 为了让您高兴，我在这里整理了七种言语的致命罪过。
transcript.english: I've assembled for your pleasure here seven deadly sins of speaking.

transcript.chinese: 我并不是假装这是一个详尽的清单，但我认为这七个是我们所有人都可能陷入的相当大的习惯。
transcript.english: I'm not pretending this is an exhaustive list, but these seven, I think, are pretty large habits that we can all fall into.

transcript.chinese: 首先，八卦。
transcript.english: First, gossip.

transcript.chinese: 说不在场的人的坏话。
transcript.english: Speaking ill of somebody who's not present.

transcript.chinese: 这不是一个好习惯，我们非常清楚那个八卦的人，五分钟后，就会八卦我们。
transcript.english: Not a nice habit, and we know perfectly well the person gossiping, five minutes later, will be gossiping about us.

transcript.chinese: 第二，判断。
transcript.english: Second, judging.

transcript.chinese: 我们知道有人在谈话中是这样的，如果你知道自己在被评判的同时又被认为有缺陷，那么你就很难倾听别人的意见。
transcript.english: We know people who are like this in conversation, and it's very hard to listen to somebody if you know that you're being judged and found wanting at the same time.

transcript.chinese: 第三，消极情绪。
transcript.english: Third, negativity.

transcript.chinese: 你可能会陷入这样的境地。
transcript.english: You can fall into this.

transcript.chinese: 我的母亲在她生命的最后几年变得非常消极，很难倾听。
transcript.english: My mother, in the last years of her life, became very negative, and it's hard to listen.

transcript.chinese: 记得有一天，我对她说：“今天是10月1日。”她说：“我知道，这不是很可怕吗？” （笑声） 当有人那么消极时，很难去倾听。
transcript.english: I remember one day, I said to her, "It's October 1 today," and she said, "I know, isn't it dreadful?" (Laughter) It's hard to listen when somebody's that negative.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）还有另一种形式的消极情绪，抱怨。
transcript.english: (Laughter) And another form of negativity, complaining.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，这就是英国的民族艺术
transcript.english: Well, this is the national art of the U.K.

transcript.chinese: 这是我们的国民运动。
transcript.english: It's our national sport.

transcript.chinese: 我们抱怨天气、体育、政治、一切，但实际上，抱怨是病毒式的痛苦。
transcript.english: We complain about the weather, sport, about politics, about everything, but actually, complaining is viral misery.

transcript.chinese: 它并没有在世界上传播阳光和光明。
transcript.english: It's not spreading sunshine and lightness in the world.

transcript.chinese: 借口。
transcript.english: Excuses.

transcript.chinese: 我们都见过这个人。
transcript.english: We've all met this guy.

transcript.chinese: 也许我们都曾经是这样的人。
transcript.english: Maybe we've all been this guy.

transcript.chinese: 有些人有一个指责者。
transcript.english: Some people have a blamethrower.

transcript.chinese: 他们只是将其传递给其他人，而不为自己的行为承担责任，同样，很难倾听这样的人。
transcript.english: They just pass it on to everybody else and don't take responsibility for their actions, and again, hard to listen to somebody who is being like that.

transcript.chinese: 倒数第二个，七个中的第六个，刺绣，夸张。
transcript.english: Penultimate, the sixth of the seven, embroidery, exaggeration.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，有时它贬低了我们的语言。
transcript.english: It demeans our language, actually, sometimes.

transcript.chinese: 例如，如果我看到一些非常棒的东西，我该怎么称呼它？
transcript.english: For example, if I see something that really is awesome, what do I call it?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）然后，当然，这种夸张就变成了谎言，我们不想听那些我们知道对我们撒谎的人。
transcript.english: (Laughter) And then, of course, this exaggeration becomes lying, and we don't want to listen to people we know are lying to us.

transcript.chinese: 最后，教条主义。
transcript.english: And finally, dogmatism.

transcript.chinese: 事实与观点的混淆。
transcript.english: The confusion of facts with opinions.

transcript.chinese: 当这两件事混为一谈时，你就会听到风声。
transcript.english: When those two things get conflated, you're listening into the wind.

transcript.chinese: 你知道，有人用他们的观点轰炸你，就好像它们是真的一样。
transcript.english: You know, somebody is bombarding you with their opinions as if they were true.

transcript.chinese: 听这个很难。
transcript.english: It's difficult to listen to that.

transcript.chinese: 这就是言语的七大罪过。
transcript.english: So here they are, seven deadly sins of speaking.

transcript.chinese: 我认为这些是我们需要避免的事情。
transcript.english: These are things I think we need to avoid.

transcript.chinese: 但有没有一种积极的方式来思考这个问题呢？
transcript.english: But is there a positive way to think about this?

transcript.chinese: 是的，有。
transcript.english: Yes, there is.

transcript.chinese: 我想建议的是，如果我们希望我们的演讲具有力量并改变世界，我们可以依靠四个真正强大的基石和基础。
transcript.english: I'd like to suggest that there are four really powerful cornerstones, foundations, that we can stand on if we want our speech to be powerful and to make change in the world.

transcript.chinese: 幸运的是，这些东西拼成一个词。
transcript.english: Fortunately, these things spell a word.

transcript.chinese: 这个词是“冰雹”，它也有一个很好的定义。
transcript.english: The word is "hail," and it has a great definition as well.

transcript.chinese: 我说的不是那些从天上掉下来砸到你头上的东西。
transcript.english: I'm not talking about the stuff that falls from the sky and hits you on the head.

transcript.chinese: 我说的这个定义是热情地打招呼或喝彩，我想如果我们站在这四件事上，我们的话就会被接受。
transcript.english: I'm talking about this definition, to greet or acclaim enthusiastically, which is how I think our words will be received if we stand on these four things.

transcript.chinese: 那么它们代表什么？
transcript.english: So what do they stand for?

transcript.chinese: 看看你能不能猜到。
transcript.english: See if you can guess.

transcript.chinese: H，当然是诚实，指的是你所说的话是真实的、直截了当、清晰的。
transcript.english: The H, honesty, of course, being true in what you say, being straight and clear.

transcript.chinese: A 是真实性，就是做你自己。
transcript.english: The A is authenticity, just being yourself.

transcript.chinese: 我的一位朋友将其描述为坚持自己的真理，我认为这是一个可爱的表达方式。
transcript.english: A friend of mine described it as standing in your own truth, which I think is a lovely way to put it.

transcript.chinese: “我”就是正直，言出必行，说到做到，成为人们可以信任的人。
transcript.english: The I is integrity, being your word, actually doing what you say, and being somebody people can trust.

transcript.chinese: L是爱。
transcript.english: And the L is love.

transcript.chinese: 我不是指浪漫的爱情，而是指祝愿人们一切顺利，原因有两个。
transcript.english: I don't mean romantic love, but I do mean wishing people well, for two reasons.

transcript.chinese: 首先，我认为绝对的诚实可能不是我们想要的。
transcript.english: First of all, I think absolute honesty may not be what we want.

transcript.chinese: 我是说，天哪，你今天早上看起来很丑。
transcript.english: I mean, my goodness, you look ugly this morning.

transcript.chinese: 也许那没有必要。
transcript.english: Perhaps that's not necessary.

transcript.chinese: 当然，用爱来磨练诚实是一件好事。
transcript.english: Tempered with love, of course, honesty is a great thing.

transcript.chinese: 而且，如果你真的希望某人好，就很难同时评判他们。
transcript.english: But also, if you're really wishing somebody well, it's very hard to judge them at the same time.

transcript.chinese: 我什至不确定你是否可以同时做这两件事。
transcript.english: I'm not even sure you can do those two things simultaneously.

transcript.chinese: 所以冰雹。
transcript.english: So hail.

transcript.chinese: 而且，现在这就是你所说的，就像那首老歌一样，这就是你所说的，这也是你说的方式。
transcript.english: Also, now that's what you say, and it's like the old song, it is what you say, it's also the way that you say it.

transcript.chinese: 你有一个很棒的工具箱。
transcript.english: You have an amazing toolbox.

transcript.chinese: 这个工具令人难以置信，但这是一个很少有人打开过的工具箱。
transcript.english: This instrument is incredible, and yet this is a toolbox that very few people have ever opened.

transcript.chinese: 我现在想和你一起翻查一下，拿出一些你可能想拿走玩的工具，这会增加你说话的力量。
transcript.english: I'd like to have a little rummage in there with you now and just pull a few tools out that you might like to take away and play with, which will increase the power of your speaking.

transcript.chinese: 例如，注册。
transcript.english: Register, for example.

transcript.chinese: 现在，假音音域在大多数情况下可能不是很有用，但在两者之间有一个音域。
transcript.english: Now, falsetto register may not be very useful most of the time, but there's a register in between.

transcript.chinese: 对于你们中的任何一位语音教练，我不会对此进行非常技术性的讨论。
transcript.english: I'm not going to get very technical about this for any of you who are voice coaches.

transcript.chinese: 不过，您可以找到您的声音。
transcript.english: You can locate your voice, however.

transcript.chinese: 所以如果我用鼻子说话，你就能听出其中的区别。
transcript.english: So if I talk up here in my nose, you can hear the difference.

transcript.chinese: 如果我把喉咙放到喉咙里，那是我们大多数人大部分时间说话的地方。
transcript.english: If I go down here in my throat, which is where most of us speak from most of the time.

transcript.chinese: 但如果你想要重量，你需要从这里到胸部。
transcript.english: But if you want weight, you need to go down here to the chest.

transcript.chinese: 你听出区别了吗？
transcript.english: You hear the difference?

transcript.chinese: 我们投票给声音较低的政客，这是事实，因为我们将深度与权力和权威联系在一起。
transcript.english: We vote for politicians with lower voices, it's true, because we associate depth with power and with authority.

transcript.chinese: 那就是注册。
transcript.english: That's register.

transcript.chinese: 然后我们就有音色了。
transcript.english: Then we have timbre.

transcript.chinese: 这就是你的声音的感觉。
transcript.english: It's the way your voice feels.

transcript.chinese: 研究再次表明，我们更喜欢丰富、流畅、温暖的声音，就像热巧克力一样。
transcript.english: Again, the research shows that we prefer voices which are rich, smooth, warm, like hot chocolate.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，如果那不是你，那也不是世界末日，因为你可以训练。
transcript.english: Well if that's not you, that's not the end of the world, because you can train.

transcript.chinese: 去找个声乐教练吧。
transcript.english: Go and get a voice coach.

transcript.chinese: 您可以通过呼吸、姿势和练习来实现一些令人惊奇的事情来改善您的音质。
transcript.english: And there are amazing things you can do with breathing, with posture, and with exercises to improve the timbre of your voice.

transcript.chinese: 然后是韵律。
transcript.english: Then prosody.

transcript.chinese: 我喜欢韵律。
transcript.english: I love prosody.

transcript.chinese: 这就是歌曲，我们用来传递意义的元语言。
transcript.english: This is the sing-song, the meta-language that we use in order to impart meaning.

transcript.chinese: 它是对话中意义的根源之一。
transcript.english: It's root one for meaning in conversation.

transcript.chinese: 那些只用一个音符说话的人，如果没有任何韵律，真的很难听。
transcript.english: People who speak all on one note are really quite hard to listen to if they don't have any prosody at all.

transcript.chinese: 这就是“单调”一词的由来，或者单调、单调。
transcript.english: That's where the word "monotonic" comes from, or monotonous, monotone.

transcript.chinese: 另外，我们现在加入了重复的韵律，每个句子都以问题结尾，而实际上它不是一个问题，而是一个陈述？
transcript.english: Also, we have repetitive prosody now coming in, where every sentence ends as if it were a question when it's actually not a question, it's a statement?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）如果你重复这个，它实际上限制了你通过韵律进行交流的能力，我认为这是一种耻辱，所以让我们尝试打破这个习惯。
transcript.english: (Laughter) And if you repeat that one, it's actually restricting your ability to communicate through prosody, which I think is a shame, so let's try and break that habit.

transcript.chinese: 步伐。
transcript.english: Pace.

transcript.chinese: 我可以通过非常快地说一些事情而变得非常兴奋，或者我可以放慢速度来强调，最后，当然，是我们的老朋友沉默。
transcript.english: I can get very excited by saying something really quickly, or I can slow right down to emphasize, and at the end of that, of course, is our old friend silence.

transcript.chinese: 谈话中保持一点沉默并没有什么问题，不是吗？
transcript.english: There's nothing wrong with a bit of silence in a talk, is there?

transcript.chinese: 我们不必用“嗯”和“啊”来填充它。
transcript.english: We don't have to fill it with ums and ahs.

transcript.chinese: 它可以非常强大。
transcript.english: It can be very powerful.

transcript.chinese: 当然，音调通常与节奏一起表示唤醒，但您可以仅使用音调来做到这一点。
transcript.english: Of course, pitch often goes along with pace to indicate arousal, but you can do it just with pitch.

transcript.chinese: 你把我的钥匙留在哪里了？
transcript.english: Where did you leave my keys?

transcript.chinese: （更高的音调）你把我的钥匙放在哪里了？
transcript.english: (Higher pitch) Where did you leave my keys?

transcript.chinese: 因此，这两次交付的含义略有不同。
transcript.english: So, slightly different meaning in those two deliveries.

transcript.chinese: 最后，音量。
transcript.english: And finally, volume.

transcript.chinese: （大声）使用音量我会感到非常兴奋。
transcript.english: (Loud) I can get really excited by using volume.

transcript.chinese: 抱歉，如果我吓到了任何人。
transcript.english: Sorry about that, if I startled anybody.

transcript.chinese: 或者，我可以通过保持安静来让你真正集中注意力。
transcript.english: Or, I can have you really pay attention by getting very quiet.

transcript.chinese: 有些人一直在广播。
transcript.english: Some people broadcast the whole time.

transcript.chinese: 尽量不要这样做。
transcript.english: Try not to do that.

transcript.chinese: 这就是所谓的 sodcasting，（笑声）把你的声音不经意地、不经意地强加给你周围的人。
transcript.english: That's called sodcasting, (Laughter) Imposing your sound on people around you carelessly and inconsiderately.

transcript.chinese: 不太好。
transcript.english: Not nice.

transcript.chinese: 当然，这一切最重要的是当你有一些非常重要的事情要做时。
transcript.english: Of course, where this all comes into play most of all is when you've got something really important to do.

transcript.chinese: 它可能会站在这样的舞台上向人们演讲。
transcript.english: It might be standing on a stage like this and giving a talk to people.

transcript.chinese: 可能是求婚、要求加薪、婚礼致辞。
transcript.english: It might be proposing marriage, asking for a raise, a wedding speech.

transcript.chinese: 不管它是什么，如果它真的很重要，你就应该看看这个工具箱和它将要工作的引擎，没有引擎在不预热的情况下可以很好地工作。
transcript.english: Whatever it is, if it's really important, you owe it to yourself to look at this toolbox and the engine that it's going to work on, and no engine works well without being warmed up.

transcript.chinese: 温暖你的声音。
transcript.english: Warm up your voice.

transcript.chinese: 实际上，让我向您展示如何做到这一点。
transcript.english: Actually, let me show you how to do that.

transcript.chinese: 大家愿意站起来一会儿吗？
transcript.english: Would you all like to stand up for a moment?

transcript.chinese: 我将向您展示我在每次演讲之前都会做的六个声音热身练习。
transcript.english: I'm going to show you the six vocal warm-up exercises that I do before every talk I ever do.

transcript.chinese: 每当你要与重要的人交谈时，请执行以下操作。
transcript.english: Any time you're going to talk to anybody important, do these.

transcript.chinese: 首先，双臂举起，深吸一口气，然后呼出，啊啊啊，就这样。
transcript.english: First, arms up, deep breath in, and sigh out, ahhhhh, like that.

transcript.chinese: 再一次。
transcript.english: One more time.

transcript.chinese: 啊啊啊，非常好。
transcript.english: Ahhhh, very good.

transcript.chinese: 现在我们要预热我们的嘴唇，我们要发出“Ba，Ba，Ba，Ba，Ba，Ba，Ba，Ba”。
transcript.english: Now we're going to warm up our lips, and we're going to go Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba.

transcript.chinese: 非常好。
transcript.english: Very good.

transcript.chinese: 现在，brrrrrrrrrrr，就像你小时候一样。
transcript.english: And now, brrrrrrrrrr, just like when you were a kid.

transcript.chinese: 呜呜。
transcript.english: Brrrr.

transcript.chinese: 现在你的嘴唇应该活跃起来了。
transcript.english: Now your lips should be coming alive.

transcript.chinese: 接下来我们要用夸张的“啦，啦，啦，啦，啦，啦，啦，啦，啦”来练习舌头。
transcript.english: We're going to do the tongue next with exaggerated la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la.

transcript.chinese: 美丽的。
transcript.english: Beautiful.

transcript.chinese: 你在这方面已经做得非常好了。
transcript.english: You're getting really good at this.

transcript.chinese: 然后，掷 R。
transcript.english: And then, roll an R.

transcript.chinese: 呃呃呃。
transcript.english: Rrrrrrr.

transcript.chinese: 这就像舌头上的香槟。
transcript.english: That's like champagne for the tongue.

transcript.chinese: 最后，如果我只能做一件事，专业人士称之为警报。
transcript.english: Finally, and if I can only do one, the pros call this the siren.

transcript.chinese: 真的很好。
transcript.english: It's really good.

transcript.chinese: 它以“我们”开头，然后到“哦”。 “我们”高，“aw”低。
transcript.english: It starts with "we" and goes to "aw." The "we" is high, the "aw" is low.

transcript.chinese: 所以你走吧，weeeaawww，weeeaawww。
transcript.english: So you go, weeeaawww, weeeaawww.

transcript.chinese: 极好的。
transcript.english: Fantastic.

transcript.chinese: 给自己热烈的掌声。
transcript.english: Give yourselves a round of applause.

transcript.chinese: 请坐，谢谢。
transcript.english: Take a seat, thank you.

transcript.chinese: （鼓掌）下次讲话的时候，提前做好这些。
transcript.english: (Applause) Next time you speak, do those in advance.

transcript.chinese: 现在让我把这个放在上下文中结束。
transcript.english: Now let me just put this in context to close.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个严肃的问题。
transcript.english: This is a serious point here.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我们现在所处的位置，对吧？
transcript.english: This is where we are now, right?

transcript.chinese: 我们对那些在充满噪音和糟糕音响效果的环境中根本不听话的人说话不太好。
transcript.english: We speak not very well to people who simply aren't listening in an environment that's all about noise and bad acoustics.

transcript.chinese: 我在这个舞台上分不同阶段讲过这个问题。
transcript.english: I have talked about that on this stage in different phases.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们对那些在真正适合目的的环境中有意识地倾听的人进行强有力的讲话，世界会是什么样子？
transcript.english: What would the world be like if we were speaking powerfully to people who were listening consciously in environments which were actually fit for purpose?

transcript.chinese: 或者说得更大一点，如果我们有意识地创造声音、有意识地消费声音、有意识地为声音设计我们所有的环境，世界会是什么样子？
transcript.english: Or to make that a bit larger, what would the world be like if we were creating sound consciously and consuming sound consciously and designing all our environments consciously for sound?

transcript.chinese: 那将是一个听起来确实很美丽的世界，一个理解将成为常态的世界，这是一个值得传播的想法。
transcript.english: That would be a world that does sound beautiful, and one where understanding would be the norm, and that is an idea worth spreading.

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。
transcript.english: Thank you.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
